user_story: As the system, I want transaction recording to be idempotent on (account, type, amount, date) so that agent retries after silent timeouts do not double-debit the user's balance.
expected_behaviour: services.record_transaction opens an atomic transaction, acquires SELECT FOR UPDATE on the FinanceAccount row, queries for an existing FinanceTransaction matching (tenant, account, transaction_type, amount, date). On a hit: returns the existing row's data with duplicate=True and created=False (no balance change). On a miss: creates the FinanceTransaction, mutates current_balance (payment/refund subtract; charge/interest add; transfer has no effect), saves the account, returns the new row with duplicate=False and created=True. Both paths return the same (payload dict, bool) tuple shape.
dependencies: FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, Django atomic transactions
inventory_notes: SELECT FOR UPDATE serialises concurrent writes per account (e.g. simultaneous agent retries). Description field is not part of the dedup key — same transaction with different description is deduplicated.

user_story: As the system, I want to capture a point-in-time monthly snapshot of each tenant's balances so that the user and the AI assistant can track debt reduction progress over time.
expected_behaviour: apps/finance/snapshot.py:create_monthly_snapshots is designed to be called on the 1st of each month (via QStash or a management command). Queries all Tenants where finance_enabled=True and status=ACTIVE. For each tenant: skips if a FinanceSnapshot with that date already exists (idempotent). Loads active accounts, computes total_debt, total_savings, and total_payments_this_month (sum of payment transactions in the previous calendar month). Creates a FinanceSnapshot with accounts_json as a point-in-time list. Returns the count of snapshots created. Per-tenant exceptions are caught and logged without aborting the run.
dependencies: Tenant model, FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, FinanceSnapshot model
inventory_notes: Not yet registered as a named QStash task in TASK_MAP (no 'monthly_finance_snapshot' entry found in cron/views.py). Covered only in tests; caller must be confirmed before relying on scheduled delivery. unique_together=(tenant, date) prevents double-write.

feature: Finance USER.md Envelope Section (Gravity Context Injection)
user_story: As the system, I want to inject the subscriber's current finance state into the assistant's USER.md workspace file so that the AI has live financial context at the start of every session.
expected_behaviour: apps/finance/envelope.py:render_finance is registered as a USER.md section with key='finance', heading='## Gravity — finance state', order=50. Enabled gate: tenant.finance_active (folds in GRAVITY_ENABLED platform pause). refresh_on=(FinanceAccount, FinanceTransaction, PayoffPlan) — section is regenerated whenever any of these models change for the tenant. Renders: active account count and total debt, the active payoff plan summary (strategy/months/payoff date/budget), top-priority debt by strategy (avalanche: highest APR; else snowball: lowest balance), upcoming due dates within 7 days (modular 31-day arithmetic), and last 3 transactions from the past 14 days. Truncates at max_chars=1000 with a note directing the agent to call nbhd_finance_summary for full state.
dependencies: FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, PayoffPlan model, apps/orchestrator/envelope_registry.py
inventory_notes: The 7-day upcoming-payment calculation uses modular day arithmetic (a.due_day - today.day) % 31, which can produce false positives near month boundaries for due_day < today.day within the same month.

feature: Finance Status Projection (Recurring Obligations / Monthly Folding)
user_story: As the system, I want to project each finance account's payment obligation for the current billing cycle so that the assistant's live status snapshot correctly shows which debts are paid and which are outstanding.
expected_behaviour: apps/journal/status_projection.py:_provide_finance is registered as a status provider for key='finance', enabled by _finance_active(tenant). Computes current_period_bounds(today) (the calendar month). Aggregates payment transaction totals per account for the current period. For each active FinanceAccount calls obligation_for_account(account, paid_this_period, today) and collects non-null obligations into {'obligations': [...]}. This data feeds into build_journal_status, which assembles the authoritative live-status dict used by proactive/cron reasoning. Finance obligations suppress FinanceAccount-linked Task objects in the tasks provider (to avoid double-reporting the same obligation via both channels).
inventory_notes: Also suppresses Journal Tasks linked to FinanceAccount via related_ref.object_type='FinanceAccount' while finance is active — prevents double-counting.

feature: Weekly Gravity Check-In Cron (OpenClaw Agent Turn)
user_story: As the system, I want to trigger a weekly Sunday-evening finance check-in for finance-enabled tenants so that the AI assistant proactively reviews the subscriber's progress and keeps them accountable.
expected_behaviour: Seeded into OpenClaw's cron registry via apps/orchestrator/config_generator.py:build_cron_seed_jobs when tenant.finance_active is True. Scheduled as a cron expression '0 19 * * 0' (Sunday 19:00) in the tenant's configured timezone. sessionTarget='isolated'. Delivery mode 'none' (no push notification). Payload prompt (_GRAVITY_WEEKLY_PROMPT) instructs the agent to call nbhd_gravity_query for current data, never assert finance numbers from memory, produce a brief check-in message (positive framing, 2-3 sentences), and send it via nbhd_send_to_user. Omitted entirely from the cron seed when tenant.finance_active is False (including when GRAVITY_ENABLED is False).
dependencies: OpenClaw Gateway API (cron registration), Tenant.finance_active, Tenant.timezone
inventory_notes: Fires at a different hour (19:00) than the weekly reflection cron (20:00) to prevent collision. Uses DEEPSEEK_FLASH_MODEL (gravity_weekly_checkin model slot).

user_story: As the system, I want to capture a weekly Gravity snapshot so that the assistant's history and comparison tools have a time-series of the user's financial state.
expected_behaviour: QStash task 'snapshot_gravity_weekly' → apps/insights/tasks.py:snapshot_gravity_weekly_task. Calls apps/insights/snapshots.py:compute_gravity_snapshot per finance-active tenant. compute_gravity_snapshot loads active accounts, active plan, and last 10 transactions; returns a schema_version=1 JSON payload with totals (debt/savings/minimums), per-account data (including payoff_progress), and recent transactions. Result is stored in a PillarSnapshot row (idempotent per ISO week). Skips hibernated tenants.
dependencies: FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, PayoffPlan model, apps/insights models (PillarSnapshot), QStash
inventory_notes: Phase 1 only. Category-level trajectory (requires a 'category' field on FinanceTransaction) is deferred to Phase 1.5.

user_story: As the system, I want to generate a weekly written reflection on the subscriber's financial progress so that the AI has a synthesized narrative to reference in future conversations.
expected_behaviour: QStash task 'weekly_gravity_reflection' → apps/insights/tasks.py:weekly_gravity_reflection_task. Hourly dispatcher; fires Django-side LLM synthesis for each tenant whose local time is Sunday 09:00 (different hour from the agent-side check-in). Calls apps/insights/synthesis.py:generate_weekly_reflection. That function short-circuits with skipped='finance_disabled' if tenant.finance_active is False. Otherwise calls an LLM (DeepSeek or configured model) with last week's Gravity data to produce a short reflection observation. Bills to platform (is_system=True), not user quota.
dependencies: QStash, LiteLLM/LLM routing, FinanceAccount model, apps/insights models, Tenant.finance_active
inventory_notes: Gated on tenant.finance_active (folds in GRAVITY_ENABLED). Platform billing (is_system=True) so it does not consume user token quota.

feature: Finance Plugin Injection into OpenClaw Config
user_story: As the system, I want to include the nbhd-finance-tools plugin in the OpenClaw configuration when Gravity is enabled so that the AI assistant has access to finance tool calls during its session.
expected_behaviour: apps/orchestrator/config_generator.py:generate_openclaw_config conditionally appends the nbhd-finance-tools plugin (ID from OPENCLAW_FINANCE_PLUGIN_ID setting, path from OPENCLAW_FINANCE_PLUGIN_PATH) to the plugins list when tenant.finance_active is True. Also conditionally includes the nbhd-insights-tools plugin for the weekly snapshot/comparison tools. Both are absent when the platform GRAVITY_ENABLED flag is False. Config is written to the tenant's Azure File Share; the container reads it on startup.
dependencies: Azure File Share (openclaw.json), Tenant.finance_active, OPENCLAW_FINANCE_PLUGIN_ID setting, OPENCLAW_FINANCE_PLUGIN_PATH setting
inventory_notes: The tool manifest is built once at container startup; a toggle takes effect only after a container restart. finance_active (not finance_enabled) is the gate, so the platform pause suppresses the plugin fleet-wide.

user_story: As an operator, I want a fail-safe-off platform-wide switch for Gravity so that I can pause the entire finance feature for all tenants while strengthening data privacy, without touching individual tenant records.
expected_behaviour: tenant.finance_active property = bool(tenant.finance_enabled) AND bool(settings.GRAVITY_ENABLED). When GRAVITY_ENABLED is False: FinanceSettingsView.patch returns HTTP 403 for any enable attempt; the finance USER.md envelope section skips rendering; the nbhd-finance-tools plugin is omitted from OpenClaw config; the Gravity Weekly Check-in cron is not seeded; TenantSerializer reports gravity_available=False; generate_weekly_reflection short-circuits. Disabling (setting finance_enabled=False) is always allowed regardless of GRAVITY_ENABLED. Production default for GRAVITY_ENABLED is False.
inventory_notes: Test and development settings set GRAVITY_ENABLED=True. The production default being False means Gravity is effectively paused in production unless explicitly enabled at the platform level.

user_story: As an operator, I want to sweep and delete duplicate FinanceTransaction rows and reverse their balance impact so that I can remediate the May 2026 agent-retry double-debit incident and correct affected account balances.
expected_behaviour: management command: python manage.py dedupe_finance_transactions [--tenant <uuid>] [--apply] [--verbose]. Dry-run by default. Finds duplicate clusters by (tenant, account, transaction_type, amount, date) where count > 1. Keeps the oldest row per cluster (by created_at, then id), collects excess row IDs. Computes per-account balance correction: payments/refunds add back amount; charges/interest subtract. Clamps proposed balance: debt accounts capped at original_balance; all accounts floored at 0. Prints per-tenant cluster/row counts and per-account balance corrections. With --apply: deletes duplicates and saves corrected balances in a single atomic transaction. Returns counts of deleted rows and adjusted accounts.
dependencies: FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, Django atomic transactions
inventory_notes: Idempotent: safe to run multiple times. --tenant restricts scope to a single tenant UUID. Dry-run output can be reviewed before committing. Balance clamping at original_balance prevents correction from overshooting the starting point for debt accounts.

user_story: As an operator, I want to view and search finance data in the Django admin so that I can inspect and troubleshoot individual tenant records.
expected_behaviour: Four ModelAdmin registrations: FinanceAccountAdmin (list: nickname, tenant, account_type, current_balance, interest_rate, is_active; filter by account_type, is_active; search by nickname and tenant__user__display_name), FinanceTransactionAdmin (list: account, transaction_type, amount, date; filter by transaction_type), PayoffPlanAdmin (list: tenant, strategy, payoff_months, total_interest, is_active; filter by strategy, is_active), FinanceSnapshotAdmin (list: tenant, date, total_debt, total_savings). Standard Django admin CRUD on all models.
dependencies: Django admin, FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, PayoffPlan model, FinanceSnapshot model
